heading: Respect for flag
label: 8 Respect for flag
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)836:176., ; , ; , , (12)–(16), .June 22, 1942, ch. 435, § 456 Stat. 379Dec. 22, 1942, ch. 806, § 456 Stat. 1076Pub. L. 94–34490 Stat. 812July 7, 1976

Amendments2024—Subsec. (c). inserted “, except as may be necessary in limited circumstances and done in a respectful manner as part of a military or patriotic observance” after “aloft and free”.Pub. L. 118–159

Modification of Department of Defense Policy, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–159, div. A, title XVII, § 1703(b)138 Stat. 2208Dec. 23, 2024“The Secretary of Defense shall—“(1)rescind the , Department of Defense memorandum entitled, ‘Clarification of Department of Defense Community Engagement Policy on Showing Proper Respect to the United States Flag’; andFebruary 10, 2023“(2)support military recruitment through public outreach events during patriotic and military observances, including the display of the United States flag regardless of size and position, including horizontally, provided that, in accordance with , United States Code, the flag never touch anything beneath it, such as the ground, the floor, water, or merchandise.”section 8(b) of title 4

Amendments2024—Subsec. (c). inserted “, except as may be necessary in limited circumstances and done in a respectful manner as part of a military or patriotic observance” after “aloft and free”.Pub. L. 118–159

Modification of Department of Defense Policy, , , provided that:Pub. L. 118–159, div. A, title XVII, § 1703(b)138 Stat. 2208Dec. 23, 2024“The Secretary of Defense shall—“(1)rescind the , Department of Defense memorandum entitled, ‘Clarification of Department of Defense Community Engagement Policy on Showing Proper Respect to the United States Flag’; andFebruary 10, 2023“(2)support military recruitment through public outreach events during patriotic and military observances, including the display of the United States flag regardless of size and position, including horizontally, provided that, in accordance with , United States Code, the flag never touch anything beneath it, such as the ground, the floor, water, or merchandise.”section 8(b) of title 4

children.label: a
children.text: The flag should never be displayed with the union down, except as a signal of dire distress in instances of extreme danger to life or property.

children.label: b
children.text: The flag should never touch anything beneath it, such as the ground, the floor, water, or merchandise.

children.label: c
children.text: The flag should never be carried flat or horizontally, but always aloft and free, except as may be necessary in limited circumstances and done in a respectful manner as part of a military or patriotic observance.

children.label: d
children.text: The flag should never be used as wearing apparel, bedding, or drapery. It should never be festooned, drawn back, nor up, in folds, but always allowed to fall free. Bunting of blue, white, and red, always arranged with the blue above, the white in the middle, and the red below, should be used for covering a speaker’s desk, draping the front of the platform, and for decoration in general.

children.label: e
children.text: The flag should never be fastened, displayed, used, or stored in such a manner as to permit it to be easily torn, soiled, or damaged in any way.

children.label: f
children.text: The flag should never be used as a covering for a ceiling.

children.label: g
children.text: The flag should never have placed upon it, nor on any part of it, nor attached to it any mark, insignia, letter, word, figure, design, picture, or drawing of any nature.

children.label: h
children.text: The flag should never be used as a receptacle for receiving, holding, carrying, or delivering anything.

children.label: i
children.text: The flag should never be used for advertising purposes in any manner whatsoever. It should not be embroidered on such articles as cushions or handkerchiefs and the like, printed or otherwise impressed on paper napkins or boxes or anything that is designed for temporary use and discard. Advertising signs should not be fastened to a staff or halyard from which the flag is flown.

children.label: j
children.text: No part of the flag should ever be used as a costume or athletic uniform. However, a flag patch may be affixed to the uniform of military personnel, firemen, policemen, and members of patriotic organizations. The flag represents a living country and is itself considered a living thing. Therefore, the lapel flag pin being a replica, should be worn on the left lapel near the heart.

children.label: k
children.text: The flag, when it is in such condition that it is no longer a fitting emblem for display, should be destroyed in a dignified way, preferably by burning.
